title: Untitled
content:
Here’s a bunch of data from an old chat gpt chat, and i watn to move it to a project instead. This chat will be all about Starving Student Movers, from LA, but they have many locations across the usa. The chat is sort of duplicated, because a added more detaisl and it went again. 


Client Overview: Starving Students Movers (Owner: Ethan Margalith)
1. Business Summary
Company Name: Starving Students Movers (SS Movers)
Industry: Moving & Storage
Services: Local and long-distance moving, storage solutions, packing, and commercial moving.
Locations: Multiple locations, including Riverside, Ventura, San Diego, San Francisco, Tucson, and potentially others.
Main Website: starvingstudents.com
Previous Domain: ssmovers.com (redirects to main domain)


More:
He has locations all over the usa, in a bunch of states
Some locations had been dormant and he lost gmb access to a bunch more
I think the company did really well in the 70’s and 80’s and took a dip, and are now trying to get their website and gmb ranking better
I think for a bunch he uses it as a leadgen tool, and just subs out the calls to other movers who do the work for instance
We’re doing a contract on 5 locations, website pages for that location, link building to them and the homepage, and managing the gmbs
We’re on a trial job to show some results in order to get a big contract
In general it’s not well run and theres’ a lot of work to do to recover the brand, by subing jobs, the standard is often less and they get bad reviews on google maps
Future ideas:
I showed his website to a really good dev here in my coworking and he says they need to overhaul the website, it looks like trash
He recommends building it not on wordpress as wrodpress is too bulky for it
He says to build some slick new design and build in an application for bookings and also an app to put on google store and apple store [marketing it as 2 x new streams of income]
He can get really good devs to do it in 1.5 months for 3k all in roughly. It’s a bit of an ask, but it would be awesome if it worked out, the website does look very dated
For quick wins we could get a reputation management system in place, maybe through a mate of mine who has basically gone down the climbo route with his own brand of reputation management saas product. - could hook all his gmbs up to it for reputation management and hitting up leads
For a real quick win, we can also put a chat bot up. This would be a lot quicker to implement with fewer headaches to get set up that a full app build and a website make over. Maybe that’s more of a mid to long term idea with him, and just push the bot for now.
We’re also thinning of doing a partnership method with apartment blocks for them, wher ewe call them pretending to be a representative of the moving company and we build rappaort and then ask if they have a “preferred moving vendor”, they say yes at the counter with the management team over the phone. And then the moving company comes over with a poster with a qr to their website or gmb. And we offer   a 10% discount on all moves done through that apartment. Win-win. Recurring client for the moving company, and the apartment people get a trusted vendor and a discount
2. Key Communication Style & Preferences
Ethan is a strong D personality (DISC profile):
Prefers direct, short, no-fluff responses.
Doesn't engage in unnecessary greetings or sign-offs.
Wants actionable insights and solutions, not long explanations.
Heavy Email User:
Sends a high volume of emails weekly, often forwarding things he doesn’t want to deal with.
Needs clear summaries of key takeaways and action points.
Doesn't Like Technical Jargon:
Struggles with SEO terms; prefers simple explanations.
Needs reports and Loom videos to be clear and concise, preferably with captions or written summaries.
3. SEO & Website Work
Ongoing Local SEO Management for 5 Locations:
Riverside, Ventura, San Diego, San Francisco, Tucson.
Focus on ranking GMBs & heatmaps.
Issues with Access:
Lost WordPress admin access at some point.
Needs web access for SEO implementations (e.g., adding pages, fixing pop-ups).
Backlink Strategy:
Previously had a strong backlink profile with ssmovers.com.
New site (starvingstudents.com) has a weaker backlink profile.
Avoids spammy link-building strategies.
Google Search Console Issues:
Duplicate URL issue with /blog/?no_redirect=true being indexed.
Old pages from ssmovers.com triggering 404s but no major SEO impact.
4. GMB Management
Active Locations:
Regular GMB posts needed for all 5 active locations.
Ensuring service area-based GMBs (SAB) are properly set.
Monitoring heatmap rankings and keyword growth.
Fresno GMB:
Ethan forwarded an email from Google about outdated posts but SS Movers is not actively managing this location.
Q&A Management:
Actively adding relevant Q&As for each GMB.
Ethan wants useful Q&As but doesn’t specify them himself.
5. Other Notable Issues & Considerations
API Issue with LeadConnector (Chat Widget):
API was invalid; might require reconnecting the external CRM.
Spam & Unnecessary Emails:
Ethan frequently receives “awards” emails that are likely pay-to-win schemes.
Regularly gets cold outreach emails for guest posts and collaborations (usually ignored).
Prefers Heatmaps for Keyword Tracking:
Uses heatmaps to measure keyword movement over traditional ranking reports.
Prioritizes 25 core keywords per location.
Prefers Actionable Fixes Over Explanations:
Wants solutions to be executed rather than long reports.
Willing to pay for results but expects transparency.
6. Response Guidelines for Ethan
Keep replies short & direct.
Summarize action points instead of explaining problems in detail.
Use simple terms instead of technical SEO jargon.
Provide solutions, not just problems.
Don’t include unnecessary greetings or sign-offs.


____________________


Starving Students Movers - Client Overview & Project Strategy
1. Client Overview
Business: Starving Students Movers
Owner: Ethan Margalith
Service: Moving company with multiple locations
Key Business Strengths: Long-established brand, well-known name in the industry
Key Weaknesses: Weak GMB performance, low review count, bad ratings on GMB
Current Contract: 3-month Local SEO contract (coming to an end)
Potential Upside: If we show results, we can expand services and charge more per location
2. Communication Style
DISC Profile: High D (Direct, results-driven, no fluff)
Preferred Communication: Short, concise, to-the-point emails (e.g., no greetings/sign-offs)
Common Interaction: Forwards emails/issues from Google Search Console, GMB updates, and expects quick solutions
Key Challenge: Must break down SEO and technical issues into clear, simple explanations
3. GMB & Local SEO Work
5 Locations: Riverside, Ventura, San Diego, Tucson, San Francisco
Heatmaps & Keywords: Tracking 25 top local keywords per location
GMB Performance:
Weak organic growth in 3 months
Low review count & poor ratings hurting rankings
Highly competitive industry where reviews matter
Ahrefs Data: Website gaining keywords and looking strong
Challenge: GMB results still weak, need proof of concept to extend contract
4. Website & Technical SEO
Current Website Issues:
Website looks outdated and poorly built
Indexing issues with Google (duplicate URLs, bad redirects, 404s)
API problems (chat widget broken, LeadConnector invalid API key)
Solution Approach:
Full Website Redesign: Bringing in proper developers to revamp the site
AI Chatbot Integration: Offering an AI bot (built by a friend) to improve lead conversion, charging $500/m for it
Technical Fixes: Ensuring all redirects, indexing issues, and broken APIs are corrected
Upsell Potential: If we fix the site and integrate AI, it gives Ethan more reason to extend and expand the contract
5. Reputation Management & Reviews
Problem: Low review count and poor ratings on GMB
Strategy:
Pushing for more customer reviews
Possibly arranging some reviews to improve scores on the lowest-rated GMBs
Monitoring review sentiment to ensure sustained reputation growth
6. Growth Strategy & Upsell Potential
Immediate Goals: Show enough SEO and technical improvements to justify extending the contract
Short-Term Strategy: Secure better GMB rankings & get more reviews to improve trustworthiness
Long-Term Play:
Expand from 5 locations to more locations under our management
Charge higher per-location SEO fees once results are clear
Sell additional services (website rebuild, AI chatbot, review management)
7. Next Steps
SEO Focus: Push for more GMB visibility, continue growing website traffic
Tech Fixes: Resolve website and chat widget issues ASAP
AI Pitch: Position chatbot as a key solution for lead generation & conversion
Review Boost: Implement small-scale reputation improvement strategy
Renewal Strategy: Use Ahrefs growth data to highlight progress and push for an extended contract
Conclusion: This project has the potential to turn into a high-value, long-term contract. While SEO results take time, the website fixes, AI chatbot, and review improvements can provide quick wins that justify Ethan keeping us on board. The focus now is on delivering enough value in the short term to secure ongoing work.
